title: Buying Bitcoin SV (BSV): Safest Exchanges and Steps
content: [Bitcoin](/bitcoin) SV (BSV) is a unique digital asset designed to prioritize massive on-chain scalability for enterprise-level applications. For those looking to gain exposure, it is critical to understand the risks, specifically noting the TokenRadar Risk Score of 8, which indicates a high-risk profile for this asset. Unlike [Bitcoin Cash](/bitcoin-cash) or [eCash](/ecash), which have different development trajectories, BSV aims to restore the original [vision](/vision-3) of the [Bitcoin](/bitcoin) protocol. To purchase BSV, you should first identify reputable, high-liquidity exchanges that list the token. Once an account is established on a supported platform, the process generally involves depositing fiat currency via bank transfer or credit card, then navigating to the BSV/FIAT trading pair. It is standard practice to use limit orders to manage your entry price in a volatile market. Once acquired, the most secure method for storage is to transfer the tokens from the exchange to a hardware wallet, which provides cold storage and protects your private keys. Keeping assets on an exchange is generally recommended only for short-term trading. Before proceeding, review all security requirements, as the loss of private keys or unauthorized access to an exchange account can lead to permanent loss of funds. Always ensure you are using an official, verified wallet client to interact with the network. As the crypto market evolves, staying updated with technical documentation on the official website is essential for all holders. By following these basic security protocols, you can effectively manage the custody of your assets.
